decisions.reason: The target is bounded, killable, and supported by H1/H6/H8. It states the no-escape result for the current Adapter_P trace class without pretending to be a universal theorem.
This conditional theorem is ready as Track 2 support: bounded formal/local OSAG support remains conditional and nonphysical.
The repo has only a bounded Adapter_P trace class, not a universe of all physical novelty.
H6 bounded formal/local support is not physical Adapter_P passage and cannot be upgraded into source issuance.
H8 is a signature bundle and explicitly does not decide the non-computable FTS/Godelian structural bit.
The packet route is not a theorem by itself, but it is the activation contract that would kill or revise the bounded theorem target.

honest_current_result: The current post-H8 record supports a bounded theorem target over the repo's Adapter_P trace class. It does not support a universal physics no-go, a D-FORK decision, physical source issuance, claim movement, or TI-C020 reopening.
next_track_1_recommendation: Mechanize or formally package the bounded completion-barrier theorem target unless a new concrete H7-admitted packet appears. If such a packet appears, route it through the counterexample obligations first.

targets.conclusion: A candidate in this bounded class does not establish source issuance unless it supplies the counterexample contract: a new concrete H7-admitted packet defeating all completion channels, the H6/H8 stops, and whole-family completion.
targets.scope: post-H8 record-preserving Adapter_P traces that carry H1 completion channels, H2/H7 preservation fields, the H6 bounded-conditional boundary, and the H8 signature bundle
targets.title: Bounded Adapter_P completion barrier

targets.conclusion: Formal/local OSAG support is internal to the bounded construction only as conditional form, not as full source structure or physical Adapter_P passage.
targets.scope: H7-admitted bounded formal/local OSAG support after H6
targets.title: Formal/local OSAG conditional theorem

targets.conclusion: No physical system can ever instantiate source issuance.
targets.scope: all physical novelty or all future physics candidates
targets.title: Universal physical no-go

targets.conclusion: Bounded formal/local OSAG support establishes physical source issuance.
targets.title: Physical source theorem from bounded OSAG

targets.conclusion: The H8 signature bundle decides whether the operative source is finite-type-space or Godelian.
targets.title: D-FORK decision from H8 signatures

targets.conclusion: A future packet can kill the bounded theorem target only by satisfying every named counterexample obligation.
targets.title: New packet counterexample contract
